_readme.rule: N/A-not-on-graph (r3sw8 pilot -> r3sw9 mechanism): an N/A-type option that means 'this metric does not apply to us' must leave the graph AND the denominator — the distribution renders over the applicable base only.

modes.conditioned: answer rows exist ONLY for the applicable base (seed-level conditioning, r3sw1/r3sw8 pattern). The declaration adds the member-facing base caption; aggregation is untouched. Enforcement of the base itself is the subset_orgs coherence pair + the tier-2d arming check.
modes.answerer_only: declared na_options leave the block at AGGREGATION: those answers drop from n and from the bars; the block carries excluded_na. Changing a declaration requires re-aggregation. Ruled per metric — this changes rendered distributions.

_readme.fields: mode; base_label (member-facing caption, lowercase mid-sentence); na_options (answerer_only only: exact option labels to exclude); parent {qid, contains|value} (conditioned only: documents the gate — the coherence pair is the enforcement)
_readme.governance: every declaration is RULED (David) before it lands. qa_release validates each declaration (active qid, known mode, na_options are real option labels, parent exists). Don't-know options are NOT this mechanism (blanket-DK diff); 'Varies'-type options stay on the graph.
_readme.env: LUMI_AB_CONFIG overrides this file's path for gate servers and staged runs — the served copy is immutable until an approved live write (r3sw7 doctrine).
